summary: High‑energy 1979 Nassau show captured on a solid audience recording, highlighted by a soaring Dark Star and stellar I Need a Miracle.
review: The performance is widely praised for its intensity, rare Dark Star, and standout moments like I Need a Miracle, Shakedown Street and the Wharf‑Rat into St. Stephen transition. The Gatto‑wise audience source provides excellent presence and mastering, though a few glitches (Dire Wolf glitch, missing drum minute) keep it from being flawless. Overall the show is considered one of the best of the early ’79 tour and the recording is among the better audience tapes available.

ai_rating.rationale: All six reviews are strongly positive (4‑5 stars) and consistently highlight both superb musical moments and a high‑quality audience recording, yielding a rating in the 4½ range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Gatto‑wise AUD offers strong presence and mastering; noted glitch in Dire Wolf and missing last‑minute drum segment, but overall clear, energetic capture of the band.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A long, 23‑song marathon that ebbs and flows beautifully; the first set builds momentum with tight numbers before a soaring second set featuring extended jams and rare selections.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers praise Jerry Garcia’s subtle yet powerful playing on I Need a Miracle and his work throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Noted as part of the last great stand of Keith (keys) & Donna; no specific performance comments beyond era context.
band_member_comments.Drums: Recording notes a missing drum minute in Dire Wolf; otherwise no explicit performance critique.
